free_text: Alpha-Particle-Induced Fission of Th230, Th232, and
The two semiconductor detectors were mounted
in a back-to-back arrangement at an angle of 90
degrees relative to the beam direction.
The targets used had areal densities of 10-30
microg/cm2 and were prepared by evaporation onto
Measurements have been made of the kinetic
energies of coincident- fission fragment pairs
fission of 230Th, 232Th, and 233U to determine the
mass and total kinetic-energy distributions of the
fragments. From a comparison of the mass
measurements with the mass distributions obtained
is possible to deduce the average number of prompt
neutrons emitted as a function of the fragment mass.
In the energy measurements the solid angle subtended
by the detectors was approximately 9x10**-2 sr.
No details on sources of uncertainties.
